rxn_class_summary: electrochemical three-component thiomethoxylation of vinyl ethers with diaryl disulfides and methanol via thiyl-radical addition/radical-polar crossover
electrochemical_role: anodic oxidation of PhSSPh (or thiolate) to generate PhS• thiyl radical that adds to the vinyl ether

key_intermediates.name: alpha-alkoxy carbon radical from PhS• addition to ethyl vinyl ether

electron_flow: Anodic oxidation generates a PhS• thiyl radical from PhSSPh; PhS• adds to the vinyl ether to give an α-alkoxy carbon radical, which is further oxidized (anodically) to an oxocarbenium ion that is trapped by MeOH to furnish the α-methoxy thioether product.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Target product formed only in trace amounts; TEMPO-SPh adduct (C15H24NOS+, m/z 266.1571, calcd 266.1573) detected by HRMS, confirming a thiyl-radical intermediate.

notes: Only the radical-trapping control experiment is shown on this page; trapping captures the PhS• rather than the carbon radical, so the downstream cation step (radical-polar crossover with MeOH) is inferred from product structure rather than directly probed here.
